acceptance: libcurl, Boost.Asio, Boost.Beast, Boost.JSON, OpenSSL, zlib, libtorrent-rasterbar, and optional pugixml are recorded as the target dependency set with reasons.
verification: overview.md Library Policy and dependency-ledger.csv record libcurl, Boost.Asio, Boost.Beast, Boost.JSON, OpenSSL, zlib, libtorrent-rasterbar, and conditional pugixml as the target set. Current source evidence shows Boost, libtorrent-rasterbar, OpenSSL, and zlib already have CMake or packaging presence; libcurl, Boost.Beast, Boost.JSON, and pugixml are selected targets for later checkpoints.
notes: pugixml is conditional on retaining Metalink.

acceptance: GnuTLS, WinTLS, nettle, libgcrypt, GMP, direct libssh2 ownership, c-ares, libuv, wslay, libxml2, and Expat have explicit remove or prune decisions.
verification: dependency-ledger.csv records remove or prune decisions for GnuTLS, WinTLS, nettle, libgcrypt, GMP, direct libssh2 ownership, c-ares, libuv, wslay, libxml2, and Expat. Current evidence confirms these still appear in cmake/modules/Options.cmake, cmake/modules/FeatureChecks.cmake, cmake/modules/Targets.cmake, cmake/Sources.cmake, README.md, and packaging/dependencies.env, so actual removal belongs to later checkpoints.
notes: Direct libssh2 can remain only as a libcurl packaging internals detail if libcurl uses it.

scope: Native code that should not be blindly replaced
acceptance: ED2K protocol and storage orchestration are retained as native aria2-next ownership with modernization boundaries.
verification: overview.md and capability-ledger.csv retain ED2K protocol as native aria2-next code on modern shared boundaries and retain storage orchestration as product-owned verified progress, resume, integrity, and control-file behavior. Prior ED2K trackers confirm native ED2K is the current protocol baseline.
notes: Do not port ED2K to unrelated client code.

acceptance: The authoritative place for every maintained release dependency version and SHA-256 hash is identified.
verification: AGENTS.md and overview.md identify packaging/dependencies.env as the authoritative maintained release dependency version and SHA-256 source. The file currently records zlib, Expat, SQLite, c-ares, libssh2, Boost, libtorrent-rasterbar, OpenSSL, GMP, libgpg-error, libgcrypt, Android NDK, and llvm-mingw; target additions and removals are owned by CM-018 after implementation checkpoints settle the final dependency closure.
notes: Dependency updates should be deliberate and verified through release packaging.

acceptance: Dependency decisions do not contradict capability decisions.
verification: overview.md, dependency-ledger.csv, and capability-ledger.csv align: ordinary URL protocols move to libcurl; runtime moves to Boost.Asio; RPC transport moves to Boost.Beast; JSON moves to Boost.JSON; TLS and crypto centralize on OpenSSL; BitTorrent stays libtorrent-rasterbar only; ED2K and storage remain native with modernized boundaries. No source deletion is claimed in CM-002.
notes: No source deletion before CM-003+ gates exist.
